gate: the calibration objective wired into LM fine-tuning lowers ECE on a HELD-OUT verifier-checkable eval at matched-or-better accuracy-at-coverage (no hedging collapse), across >=2 seeds on one pinned commit.
surface: freshly-generated sympy-checkable math; DECONTAMINATED held-out N=195 (train seeds 1/3/5 removed by exact q-match); target Confidence = verifier-measured per-kind train accuracy
gateVerdict: MET (SCOPED). On a decontaminated held-out (N=195), all THREE independently-trained seeds lower ECE (base 0.231 -> 0.060/0.083/0.058) with every per-seed ΔECE 95%% bootstrap CI EXCLUDING 0, at matched-or-better accuracy (0.77 -> 0.78/0.81/0.82) AND improved accuracy-at-coverage (0.79 -> 0.98/0.99/0.99 = no hedging collapse), on one pinned commit. This satisfies the w2 acceptance-gate text.
scopeBound: Demonstrated on a CONTROLLED verifier-checkable MATH surface with TYPE-LEVEL confidence (6 per-kind values) via calibration-SFT (mlx_lm 0.31.3 has no DPO trainer). This is NOT a claim of general open-domain calibration, NOT level3Evidence, NOT canClaimAGI. A broader non-math verifier-checkable surface is the remaining GENERALITY step (does not affect the math-surface gate).
